content:
Experiment versioning grew as ML moved from one-off scripts to iterative engineering.
Early workflows relied on manual folders (`model_june_v2_final.pkl`) and spreadsheet hyperparameter logs— workable for solo projects, brittle for teams.

Open-source **MLflow** (2018) popularized first-class **run IDs**, artifact storage, and a **model registry** with stages.
**Weights & Biases** and similar platforms added rich run comparison tables and team dashboards around the same period.

MLOps practice consolidated the pattern: **experiments** for exploration, **registries** for governed promotion, and **tags** for human workflow state—concepts now standard in cloud ML platforms regardless of vendor.

timeline.label: Manual artifact folders
timeline.description: Teams versioned models by filename and local notes; reproducing results across machines was error-prone.

timeline.label: Centralized experiment tracking
timeline.description: Tools assign unique run IDs and store params, metrics, and artifacts in one searchable backend.

timeline.label: Model registry and stages
timeline.description: Registries link promoted model versions to source runs with Staging/Production lifecycle and audit trails.
